essence: Capital-intensive, scale-driven manufacturing where volume + capacity utilisation + platform sharing drive cost and profit.
A cyclical, big-ticket, heavily financing-dependent purchase; brand, dealer network, and after-sales matter.
The EV + software transition is reshaping the industry - powertrain, the battery/chip supply chain, and the 'software-defined vehicle'.
Margins are thin on mass vehicles; profit pools shift to premium, financing, components, and increasingly software/services.
India is a global small-car + two-wheeler + components hub; supply chain (chips, batteries) and dealer/service networks are critical.

definition.oneLiner: Companies that design, manufacture, and sell vehicles (and their components) - cars, two-wheelers, trucks - plus the financing and service around them.
definition.jobToBeDone: Move people and goods - giving buyers reliable, affordable, aspirational mobility, financed and serviced over the vehicle's life.

valueChain.profitPools: Profit pools sit in premium/large vehicles, in financing, and in high-value components/aftermarket; mass-market vehicles are thin-margin and scale-dependent; software/connected services are an emerging pool.
valueChain.powerShift: Value is shifting from ICE hardware + dealer sales -> EV + batteries + software-defined vehicles -> connected services, financing, and (eventually) mobility/autonomy, as the powertrain and supply chain are rebuilt.

unitEconomics.unitOfRevenue: Revenue per vehicle sold (plus financing & aftermarket per vehicle)
unitEconomics.revenueDrivers: Units x Price. Growth comes from volume (capacity utilisation, demand, financing), price/premiumisation/mix, and aftermarket + financing attached per vehicle.

costStructure.shape: Very high fixed costs (plants, R&D, tooling) + large variable material/component cost (steel, chips, batteries); it's a capacity-utilisation game - margins swing sharply with volume and mix, and material/FX costs squeeze.
costStructure.leverage: Strong operating leverage on capacity utilisation and platform sharing (spread R&D/tooling over more units); but a demand dip or chip/battery shortage hits hard, and the EV transition adds heavy new R&D/capex.

competitionAndMoats.concentration: Globally a moderately concentrated set of large OEMs + Chinese EV entrants; India: a few dominant players per segment (Maruti in cars; Hero/Honda/TVS/Bajaj in two-wheelers; Tata/Mahindra in SUVs/CVs).

structuralForces.regulation: Heavy - emissions/safety norms (BS6, CAFE), EV incentives (FAME/PLI), import duties, scrappage policy; regulation is steering the whole EV transition.
structuralForces.cyclicality: Highly cyclical - tied to GDP, incomes, interest rates (financing), and fuel prices; also exposed to commodity and chip/battery supply cycles.
structuralForces.substitution: EVs substituting ICE; public transport, ride-hailing, and (long-term) shared/autonomous mobility reduce ownership; two-wheelers vs cars in India.
structuralForces.supplierBuyerPower: Chip and battery suppliers gained huge power (shortages); steel/commodity prices swing costs; dealers and fleet/corporate buyers negotiate; consumers are price- and finance-sensitive.

aiPenetration.maturity: Medium-to-high - AI in manufacturing, ADAS/autonomy, and increasingly the software-defined vehicle.
aiPenetration.valueChainImpact: AI is moving value from hardware to software - ADAS, connected features, OTA-monetisable services - while cutting manufacturing cost and improving quality; it is central to the EV/autonomy race.
threatsDisruption: Commoditisation of ICE as value shifts to software/batteries

ranges: OEM EBITDA margin: ~8-15% (mass); higher for premium
Two-wheeler margins: often higher than mass cars
EV share: rising fast but still a low base (India)
Component makers: margins vary widely by criticality
benchmarks.note: DIRECTIONAL - rules of thumb, not audited figures. Verify before quoting.

keyTerms.OEM: Original Equipment Manufacturer - the vehicle maker (vs component suppliers).
keyTerms.Platform: A shared vehicle architecture used across models to spread cost.
keyTerms.Capacity utilisation: How fully plants are used - key to margins.
keyTerms.ICE vs EV: Internal Combustion Engine vs Electric Vehicle.
keyTerms.ADAS: Advanced Driver-Assistance Systems - the path to autonomy.
keyTerms.SDV: Software-Defined Vehicle - features delivered/updated via software (OTA).
keyTerms.TCO: Total Cost of Ownership - key to EV vs ICE buying decisions.

questionsToAskFirst: Volume vs mix-led growth, and market-share trend?
Where in the demand cycle are we?
rookieTraps: Ignoring cyclicality (judging peak-cycle margins as normal)
Underrating the EV/software transition capex & disruption
Treating OEM and component economics as the same

whatsUniqueHere: Automotive is a brutal capital-and-scale game in the middle of its biggest reinvention in a century: a cyclical, thin-margin hardware business where capacity utilisation and platform sharing decide profitability - now upended by the shift to EVs and software-defined vehicles, which rewrites the powertrain, the supply chain (batteries, chips), and where value is captured (from steel to software). Incumbents' dealer networks and brands are real moats, but Chinese EV and software-first players are attacking exactly those.

indiaLandscape.marketStructure: A huge, fast-growing market and global manufacturing hub: Maruti Suzuki dominates passenger cars, Tata and Mahindra lead SUVs/EVs/CVs, Hero/Honda/TVS/Bajaj rule two-wheelers; a deep auto-components industry exports globally; EV adoption rising (esp. two/three-wheelers) with FAME/PLI support.
indiaLandscape.note: Semi-perishable - players & policy specifics refresh periodically.

similarTo: Consumer durables - big-ticket, financed, dealer + service network

currentState.note: PERISHABLE - refresh via research before quoting. Figures approximate.
currentState.marketStructure: India auto: SUV premiumisation and two-wheeler recovery driving growth; EV adoption accelerating in 2W/3W and rising in cars (Tata leading car EVs); global OEMs and Chinese EV interest eyeing India; PLI/FAME shaping localisation; the software-defined-vehicle race beginning.
